items.prompt:
Read the sentence and the directions that follow.
Participants in the Rokkaku battle were allowed to repair their kites before they competed in the next round.
Which detail from the passage best supports this conclusion.

options.text: "The kite flyers are chasing each other around the field, each trying to wrap his or her kite string, or ‘line,’ around someone else's"
Andrew’s line is tangled around the other kite’s, and he begins to saw his line back and forth. The other kite wavers, then its line breaks!"
"This competition has three 'heats,' or rounds. The same five kites compete in each heat."
"After all three heats, the judges add all the points together to determine the final winner."

items.prompt:
The following question has two parts. First, answer part A. Then, answer part B.

Part A

Choose the statement that best describes what the information presented in “Battle in the Sky!” shows about the author’s point of view.

options.text: It shows that the author thinks a team can be helpful to choose the design of the kite.
It shows that the author thinks a team can be helpful to divide up the responsibilities.
It shows that the author thinks a team can be helpful to make repairs on the kites after battles.
It shows that the author thinks a team can be helpful to give everyone a break from being the pilot.

items.prompt:
Part B

Which sentence from the passage best supports your answer in part A?

options.text: "Modern kites, however, might be decorated with any design, including animals, insects, flags, or funny faces."
"The other kite wavers, then its line breaks!"
"After all three heats, the judges add all the points together to determine the final winner."
"The pilot and copilot work together to fly the kite, and the scorekeeper helps keep track of the points."

items.prompt:
The following question has two parts. First, answer part A. Then, answer part B.

Part A

Which sentence best describes the author’s message?

options.text: Since the kites for Rokkaku battles are larger than regular kites, teamwork is necessary in order to win.
Although kite flying seems like a simple and easy task, Rokkaku battles require a lot of strength and skill.
Andrew Albosta, who has been flying kites since he was a kid, is the best Rokkaku kite flyer in Washington, D.C.
The 2012 Blossom Kite Festival Rokkaku Battle in Washington, D.C., allows kite flyers to participate in many battle rounds.

items.prompt:
Part B

Which sentence or sentences from the passage best supports your answer in part A?

options.text: “Down on the ground, the kite flyers begin to saw their lines back and forth, trying to pull each other’s kites out of the sky.”
“But the battle is not over yet. Just like a game of rock-paper-scissors, it’s the best out of three.”
“For this battle, each team must have four members: a pilot, a copilot, a launcher, and a scorekeeper.”
“It’s a close contest, but Andrew wins a third and final time, making him the first-place winner of the 2012 Blossom Kite Festival Rokkaku Battle.”

items.prompt:
Read the sentences from the passage.
Down on the ground, the kite flyers begin to saw their lines back and forth, trying to pull each other’s kites out of the sky. “We have engagement!” shouts the announcer. The audience cheers.
The word engagement has multiple meanings. What does the author tell the reader with the use of the word engagement? Pick two choices.

options.text: The kites are ready to fly.
The kites are in a competition.
The kites are in a specific place.
The kites are battling each other.
The kites are tangled up in the sky.

items.prompt: Which sentence gives the best conclusion about why it is important to be the last kite in the air.

options.text: The last kite in the air has a better chance over the other kites of becoming the final winner in the end.
The last kite in the air is usually the strongest and biggest kite, making it the best one in the competition.
The last kite in the air does not need to use as many supplies because it does not break its line, or string.
The last kite in the air does not have to do as much work because the other kites have left the competition.
